vulnerability_summary: ReDoS vulnerability in cross-spawn caused by improper input sanitization in argument escaping logic. Crafted input strings cause catastrophic backtracking in a regex, crashing the application.
fix_description: Fix improves regex handling in escapeArgument function to prevent catastrophic backtracking. Fixed in 7.0.5 and 6.0.6.
collaborator_reasoning: escapeArgument is use to stop the backtracking.

vulnerability_summary: Cookie HTTP header not stripped during cross-origin redirects, potentially leaking sensitive authentication cookies to unintended third-party hosts.
fix_description: Adds Cookie to DEFAULT_REMOVE_HEADERS_ON_REDIRECT list in the Retry class, ensuring Cookie headers are stripped on cross-origin redirects. Fixed in v1.26.17 and v2.0.6.
collaborator_reasoning: The Retry class is where DEFAULT_REMOVE_HEADERS_ON_REDIRECT was modified to strip Cookie headers, matching the fix directly.

vulnerability_summary: Sandbox escape in Jinja2 where the |attr filter bypasses sandboxed environment attribute lookup restrictions, allowing execution of arbitrary Python code. NOTE: The advisory primarily concerns the do_attr function (|attr filter), not do_reverse.
fix_description: Fix modifies do_attr in src/jinja2/filters.py to ensure |attr filter delegates to environment's attribute lookup and sandbox security checks. Fixed in Jinja2 v3.1.6.
collaborator_reasoning: The advisory's own note says the primary fix is in do_attr, not do_reverse; the extracted function isn't the one that gates the sandbox check.

vulnerability_summary: ReDoS vulnerability caused by inefficient regex /,.*}/ in expand() function susceptible to catastrophic backtracking on crafted long input strings.
fix_description: Replaces vulnerable regex with more performant version adding negative lookahead to prevent excessive backtracking.
collaborator_reasoning: expand() is the function containing the vulnerable regex and is the one rewritten to add the negative lookahead.

vulnerability_summary: Prototype pollution: attacker can inject properties onto Object.prototype via crafted CLI arguments (e.g. --__proto__.polluted=true), leading to DoS or property injection.
fix_description: Adds checks to prevent setting properties on __proto__, constructor, and prototype keys during argument parsing. Fixed in v1.2.2/v0.2.1 (later more complete fix in v1.2.6+).
collaborator_reasoning: hasKey is the guard function directly modified to block writes to __proto__/constructor/prototype during argument parsing.

vulnerability_summary: Prototype pollution in lodash's _.unset and _.omit: crafted paths (e.g. __proto__.toString) delete properties from global prototypes. Internal baseUnset function lacked path validation.
fix_description: baseUnset updated to block access to __proto__ and constructor before unset/omit. NOTE: 'Ye' is a minified identifier from lodash.min.js — not a named source function.
collaborator_reasoning: The summary explicitly flags Ye as a minified identifier from lodash.min.js, not a semantically meaningful source function.

vulnerability_summary: Authorization header not stripped on cross-origin redirects (different host/port/scheme), exposing credentials to unintended third-party hosts.
fix_description: Adds logic to detect cross-origin redirects and strip sensitive headers (especially Authorization) before following. Fixed in urllib3 1.23.
collaborator_reasoning: urlopen is the request path where the Authorization-header-stripping logic for cross-origin redirects was added.

vulnerability_summary: ReDoS in browserslist v4.0.0–4.16.4: crafted query string causes catastrophic regex backtracking in the query parser (including the sinceQuery path), hanging the application.
fix_description: Modifies regexes in query parsing logic to eliminate catastrophic backtracking patterns. Fixed in v4.16.5.
collaborator_reasoning: sinceQuery is one path touched by the regex fix, but the fix spans the broader query parser rather than being specific to this single function.

vulnerability_summary: CRLF injection: attacker controlling the HTTP request method can inject CR/LF characters into putrequest() (called by connect/request code paths), enabling HTTP request smuggling or header injection.
fix_description: Introduces regex-based validation in src/urllib3/connection.py to check HTTP method for CRLF control characters and raises ValueError if found. Fixed in v1.25.9.
collaborator_reasoning: connect() is where the CRLF-injection-prone HTTP method reaches putrequest(), and the fix's regex validation sits in this code path.

vulnerability_summary: ReDoS in micromatch's braces() function in index.js: greedy .* pattern causes excessive backtracking on malicious payloads with unclosed brackets. isEmptyString check is part of the affected code path.
fix_description: Tightens the greedy .* regex in brace expansion logic to prevent catastrophic backtracking. Fixed in v4.0.8.
collaborator_reasoning: isEmptyString is noted as merely part of the affected code path, not the function containing the greedy backtracking pattern in braces().

vulnerability_summary: NO_PROXY hostname normalization bypass: Axios uses literal string comparison instead of normalizing hostnames, allowing SSRF via trailing-dot hostnames (localhost.) or bracketed IPv6 ([::1]).
fix_description: Introduces normalizeNoProxyHost() in lib/helpers/shouldBypassProxy.js to strip trailing dots and IPv6 brackets before NO_PROXY comparison. Adds hardcoded loopback set. Fixed in v1.15.0 and v0.31.0.
collaborator_reasoning: isLoopbackHost is adjacent to the real fix (normalizeNoProxyHost in shouldBypassProxy.js) but isn't the function that was actually rewritten.

vulnerability_summary: Multipart form data parser accepts unlimited parts, allowing DoS via crafted request with massive number of parts causing CPU/memory exhaustion. NOTE: The vulnerability is in the multipart parser — handle_websocket_exception is in a different code path (WebSocket error handling).
fix_description: Introduces limits on number of multipart parts the parser processes. Fixed in Werkzeug 2.2.3.
collaborator_reasoning: The vulnerability and fix are in the multipart parser; handle_websocket_exception is explicitly noted as a different code path.

vulnerability_summary: Decompression bomb DoS: urllib3's streaming API fully decompresses highly compressed payloads in a single operation even when only a small chunk is requested, causing massive memory allocation. 18 functions extracted from this commit.
fix_description: Fix bounds decompression to requested chunk size, preventing decompression bombs. Fixed in urllib3 2.6.0.
collaborator_reasoning: _init_decoder is part of the decompression flow but the actual fix bounds output at the chunk-request level, not inside this initializer.

vulnerability_summary: Same advisory as sample #9: Authorization header not stripped on cross-origin redirects. getlist is an HTTPHeaderDict accessor method — 7 functions were extracted from this commit.
fix_description: Core fix is in redirect-handling logic to strip Authorization headers on cross-origin redirects. getlist is a header dictionary accessor.
collaborator_reasoning: etlist is a generic HTTPHeaderDict accessor, not the redirect-handling logic where the Authorization-header fix was made.

vulnerability_summary: NOTE: This GHSA ID may map to a Mozilla PDF.js vulnerability (font loader eval injection), not an axios issue. kindOf is a generic typeof wrapper in axios. 33 functions were extracted from a large refactoring commit.
fix_description: If this is an axios advisory: the fix would be in mergeConfig to prevent __proto__ injection. kindOf is a utility unrelated to the security fix. If PDF.js: entirely unrelated package.
collaborator_reasoning: kindOf is a generic typeof wrapper unrelated to the actual mergeConfig fix, and the advisory itself may not even be an axios issue
